title: phacelurus zea
intro: Phacelurus zea, also known as wild corn or teosinte, is a wild ancestor of domesticated corn and a type of grass native to Mexico and Central America. The medicinal properties of Phacelurus zea include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Phacelurus zea include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and provide relief from digestive issues such as constipation. Herbal preparations of Phacelurus zea can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and health.
